reasoning: Summer McIntosh holds the world record in the 400m individual medley with a time of 4:24.38, which she set in May 2024. This time is several seconds faster than her closest active competitors (e.g., Kaylee McKeown, Katie Grimes), making it extremely unlikely that another swimmer will surpass her record in 2025. If McIntosh breaks her own world record, she will still be the record holder (even if the new record is pending ratification and marked with a '#'). The primary risk of her losing the record would be a doping suspension stripping her of the title, which has a very low base rate, or an unexpected phenomenal swim by a competitor. The 2025 World Aquatics Championships take place in late July to August, meaning the first three dates occur before the primary international peak-performance meet, drastically limiting opportunities for others to challenge the record. Therefore, the probabilities remain very high across all resolution dates, with slight decreases to account for increasing uncertainty over time.
